title: How to Survive Solar Plexus Emotions

body:
How to Survive Solar Plexus Emotions

You're here, aren't you? It's probably late. Maybe it's 3 AM, and you're staring at the ceiling, or maybe you're curled up in a ball, stomach churning, mind racing. You feel like you're losing your goddamn mind, and no one understands. Your solar plexus, that spot right above your belly button, it's screaming. It's a knot of fear, anxiety, rage, grief - sometimes all at once. And you're pretty sure it's not just regular anxiety, right? You've tried all the breathing exercises, the meditations, the "just think positive" bullshit. It's not working. You're feeling something deeper, something primal, something that feels like it could tear you apart from the inside. I know that feeling. I've been there. I've lived through it. And I'm telling you, you're not alone, and you're not crazy. You're probably going through a spiritual emergency, a kundalini awakening, and those solar plexus emotions are a huge, terrifying part of it.

I'm Kalesh. I went through my own hell, a full-blown kundalini crisis that felt like I was being burned alive from the inside out. My solar plexus was a constant battlefield. Panic attacks that felt like heart attacks, waves of terror that would grip me for hours, anger so intense I felt like I'd explode, and grief that felt like a gaping wound. Doctors told me it was anxiety, depression, bipolar. Therapists tried to medicate me. No one, and I mean no one, understood that this wasn't just a mental health issue. This was a soul-level upheaval, and those solar plexus emotions were the epicenter.

So, you're feeling that churning, that intense sensation in your gut. It's not just a feeling; it's a physical sensation, isn't it? It's like a coiled snake, a burning fire, a constant pressure. You might be having digestive issues, nausea, loss of appetite, or even intense hunger that never quite satisfies. Your body is reacting to the immense energetic shifts happening within you. This isn't just "stress." This is your deepest wounds, your oldest traumas, your inherited patterns - all of it getting churned up. It's like a cosmic washing machine, and your solar plexus is the agitator. It's where your personal power resides, your sense of self, your boundaries, your will. When that area gets activated in a big way, everything that has ever compromised those things comes roaring to the surface. It's terrifying. I won't lie to you. But it's also a sign that something deeply important is happening. You're not breaking; you're breaking open.

What's Happening in Your Solar Plexus?

Okay, let's get real about what's going on down there. Your solar plexus, or Manipura chakra in yogic traditions, is your power center. It's where you process emotions related to your identity, your self-esteem, your personal power, and your autonomy. When kundalini energy starts to move, or when you're in a spiritual emergency, this area gets supercharged. It's like turning on a high-powered scanner that's suddenly looking at every single energetic blockage, every unexpressed emotion, every unresolved conflict you've ever had related to who you are and your place in the world. And it's doing it with the intensity of a thousand suns, or at least it feels that way, right?

You're not just "feeling" angry; you're feeling the suppressed anger from every time you were told to shut up, to be small, to not express yourself. You're not just "feeling" anxious; you're feeling the fear of not being good enough, the terror of judgment, the deep-seated worry that you're at its core flawed. This isn't just your current life's baggage. It can be ancestral trauma, past life stuff - things you can't even consciously remember but your body and energy system certainly do. It's all stored there, in that gut space. And now, it's being brought up for release. It's messy. It's painful. It's often accompanied by physical symptoms that make you think you're having a heart attack or an ulcer. I've been there, thinking I was dying, only for every medical test to come back "normal." It's infuriating, isn't it?

Here's the thing: these aren't just random, unpleasant feelings. They're messages. They're energetic patterns that are finally getting enough light and pressure to be seen and processed. Your system is trying to clear itself out. It's like a deep clean of your energetic hard drive. And just like a computer cleanup, it can be slow, noisy, and sometimes crash your system entirely for a bit. But it's necessary. You can't build a new, stronger foundation without clearing out the old, unstable one. Know what I mean?

The Spectrum of Solar Plexus Emotions

You might be experiencing a whole range of things. Here are some of the common ones I've felt and seen in others:

  • Intense Fear and Anxiety: This isn't your garden-variety worry. This is existential dread. Panic attacks that come out of nowhere and feel like they'll never end. A constant hum of terror beneath everything you do.
  • Rage and Anger: Unexplained bursts of anger, feeling like you could snap. Resentment boiling over from seemingly minor things. This is often suppressed anger from childhood or past injustices finally making its way out.
  • Grief and Sadness: Deep, sometimes inconsolable sadness. Crying spells that feel endless. Grieving for things you can't quite name, for a past self, for perceived losses, for the state of the world.
  • Shame and Guilt: Feeling by nature bad or wrong. Ruminating on past mistakes. A deep sense of unworthiness that seems to come from nowhere.
  • Powerlessness and Victimhood: Feeling like you have no control over anything, like you're at the mercy of forces bigger than you. This is often a reflection of where your personal power has been given away or taken from you.

Does that land? Because if it does, you're not alone. These are all part of the process. They're not signs that you're falling apart; they're signs that you're being put back together, piece by excruciating piece.

Practical Tools for Survival

So, what do you do when your solar plexus is a raging inferno? You can't just ignore it. You can't just meditate it away. You need to meet it, acknowledge it, and learn to move with it, not against it. This isn't about fixing it; it's about enduring it and allowing it to move through you.

Grounding, Grounding, Grounding

This is your lifeline. When you feel those emotions rising, your energy is often shooting up and out, leaving you unmoored and terrified. You need to pull that energy back down into your body, into the earth. It's not a cure, but it's a damn good way to keep from spiraling into complete dissociation or panic.

  • Physical Contact with Earth: Get outside. Put your bare feet on the grass, the dirt, the sand. Feel the solid ground beneath you. Even if it's cold, do it for a few minutes. Imagine roots growing from your feet deep into the earth.
  • Heavy Blankets/Weighted Blankets: The pressure can be incredibly soothing. It gives your nervous system something tangible to feel, a boundary. It can mimic a hug, which you probably desperately need.
  • Sensory Input: Hold an ice cube, splash cold water on your face, sniff something strong like peppermint oil. These can snap you back into your body when your mind is racing.
  • Slow, Deep Belly Breathing: Not the shallow chest breathing you're probably doing when you're panicked. Focus on making your exhale longer than your inhale. Breathe into your belly, really feel your abdomen expand and contract. This stimulates your vagus nerve and helps calm your nervous system.
  • Eat Real Food: Forget the junk. Your body is working overtime. It needs nutrient-dense, grounding foods. Root vegetables, healthy fats, protein. Don't skip meals, even if you don't feel like eating. Small, frequent meals can help keep your blood sugar stable, which is critical when your nervous system is already on high alert.

I know it sounds simple, almost too simple for the hell you're going through. But trust me, these small, consistent actions make a huge difference in managing the intensity. They won't make it disappear, but they'll make it survivable.

Allowing and Releasing

This is the hardest part. Your instinct is to fight these feelings, to push them away, to distract yourself. But these emotions are demanding to be felt. They're old energy, stuck energy, and they need to move. Trying to suppress them is like trying to hold a beach ball underwater - it takes immense effort, and eventually, it's going to burst up anyway.

  • Feel It Without Judgment: Find a safe space. Lie down. Put your hand on your solar plexus. And just feel. Don't try to label it, don't try to understand it, don't try to change it. Just allow the sensation to be there. Imagine it as a color, a texture, a temperature. Just observe it. It sounds impossible, I know. But the act of just allowing, without resistance, can actually soften its grip.
  • Vocalization: Sometimes, these emotions need a sound. A groan, a sigh, a scream into a pillow, a roar. Don't hold it in. Your body is trying to release, and sound is a powerful release valve.
  • Movement: If you feel the urge to move, move. Shake, dance, walk, punch a pillow. Get that energy out of your body. It doesn't have to be graceful; it just has to be movement that honors what your body is trying to do.
  • Journaling - No Filter: Don't censor yourself. Write down every single dark, ugly, terrifying thought and feeling that comes up. No one has to read it. Burn it afterward if you want. The act of externalizing it can create a little space between you and the emotion.
  • Connect with Water: Take a long bath or shower. Imagine the water washing away the intensity. It's cleansing, both physically and energetically.

I've spent hours on the floor, just letting the fear wash over me, crying until I thought there were no tears left. It's brutal. But every time, eventually, it would recede. It doesn't mean it's gone forever, but it means you've moved some of it. You're teaching your system that it's safe to feel these things, safe to release them. This is the The Stormy Search for the Self by Stanislav & Christina Grof (paid link). It's a journey into the depths, and you're doing it. You really are.

Seeking Support (The Right Kind)

Look, you can't do this alone. Or rather, you shouldn't have to. But you need the right kind of support. Most conventional systems aren't equipped for what you're going through. They'll pathologize it, medicate it, and try to suppress it, which is the absolute worst thing you can do when your system is trying to process and release.

  • Find Someone Who Gets It: This is crucial. Seek out spiritual counselors, energy healers, or therapists who have experience with spiritual emergence, kundalini awakenings, or shamanic crises. Someone who won't tell you you're crazy. Someone who understands that these intense emotions are part of a larger process. Books like The Kundalini Experience by Lee Sannella (paid link) or When Spirit Leaps: Navigating the Process of Spiritual Awakening by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) can help you find language for what you're experiencing, and Greenwell also wrote The Kundalini Guide by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link), which is another good read.
  • Build a Small, Trusted Circle: You don't need to tell everyone. In fact, you shouldn't. Find one or two people - a close friend, a family member - who you can be completely honest with, even if they don't fully understand. Just having someone listen without judgment can be a lifesaver.
  • Set Boundaries: This is non-negotiable. You are going through a deep process. You don't have the energy for drama, for people who drain you, or for situations that compromise your peace. Say no. Protect your energy fiercely.
  • Self-Compassion: This is a marathon, not a sprint. You're going to have good days and really, really bad days. You're going to feel like you're failing. You're going to feel like you're not making progress. Be kind to yourself. You're doing incredibly difficult work. Treat yourself like you would a beloved child who is suffering.

I know it's hard to trust anyone when you feel this exposed and vulnerable. But finding that one person, that one resource, that one book that validates your experience - it can make all the difference. It tells you you're not alone, and that's a powerful antidote to the isolation these experiences often bring.

The Long Haul - Integration, Not Eradication

You won't "get rid of" these solar plexus emotions entirely. That's not the goal. The goal is to integrate them, to understand their messages, to release the charge around them, and to reclaim the power that was once tied up in them. This is a process of becoming more whole, not of becoming emotionless.

Over time, as you allow these emotions to move, as you ground, as you seek support, you'll notice shifts. The intensity might lessen. The duration might shorten. You might start to feel moments of peace, even joy, amidst the chaos. These are glimpses of the new you, the more expanded, authentic you, emerging from the fire. You're not just surviving; you're transforming.

It's not easy. It's the hardest thing you'll ever do. But I promise you, on the other side of this, there's a depth of peace, a clarity of purpose, and a strength you never knew you possessed. You're building a new foundation, one that's rooted in your true self, not in the fears and limitations you've carried. Keep going. You've got this. I'm telling you, you're stronger than you think. And you're not alone in the dark. I've been there. I'm still here. And you will be too.

This is not a substitute for professional mental health care. If you're in crisis, please reach out to a qualified therapist or call 988.
author: Kalesh
